title: Xylophonic Spicy Green Korean Red Lentils Kalguksu Copper Pot Jjigae
description: A peppery, gooey, and adventurous Korean dish from the Jeolla region featuring green red lentils, ssam-wrap, and a copper pot.
instructions: Heat a copper pot over medium-high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of toasted sesame oil and swirl it around.
Add 1 cup of green red lentils and cook for 2 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add 2 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the lentils are tender.
While the lentils cook, heat another copper pot over medium heat.
Add 2 tablespoons of gochujang and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add 1/2 cup of Korean chopped green onions and cook for another minute, stirring frequently.
Add 1 teaspoon of Korean chili flakes and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Add 1/2 teaspoon of salt and 1/4 teaspoon of black pepper, and stir to combine.
Add 2 cloves of minced garlic and cook for 30 seconds, stirring constantly.
Add 1 tablespoon of fish sauce and stir to combine.
Add the cooked lentils to the pot and stir to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
To assemble the dish, place a sheet of ssam wrap on a flat surface and spoon the lentil mixture onto the center of the wrap.
Fold the bottom half of the wrap up over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the wrap into a tight cylinder.
Repeat with the remaining ingredients and serve immediately.
